title: Aechmea corallina
intro: Aechmea corallina, also known as the Coral Tillandsia, is a type of epiphytic bromeliad plant that is native to tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aechmea corallina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aechmea corallina is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
taxonomy: Aechmea corallina is classified within the family Bromeliaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the pineapple family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Aechmea corallina belonging to the subfamily Tillandsioideae. The genus Aechmea itself is a group of epiphytic plants, which are plants that grow on other plants, such as trees, for support. Aechmea corallina is a species of air plant, which is a type of epiphyte that obtains moisture and nutrients from the air. Its classification is also sometimes used to discuss the broader relationships within the Bromeliaceae family.

constituents: Aechmea corallina contains active constituents that include bromelain, a mixture of enzymes with anti-inflammatory properties. These enzymes can break down proteins, making it useful for treating digestive issues and reducing swelling. Bromelain is also known for its ability to prevent blood clots and promote wound healing.
